rationale_for_baseline_tier: Functional constipation is normally low-acuity self-care, but 6 days of absolute constipation with severe cramping in a patient who cannot be examined remotely warrants prompt (24–48h) review to confirm the benign picture and exclude obstruction, alongside starting a laxative (T2). Obstruction/peritonism carries a hard ED (T4) safety-net; alarm features route to GP/colonoscopy.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Complete stopping of flatus, vomiting (especially green/bilious or foul), severe constant worsening pain, or a rigid/very distended abdomen
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests mechanical bowel obstruction / perforation — surgical emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you stop passing wind altogether, start vomiting (especially green or foul), or the pain becomes severe and constant with a hard, swollen belly — go to the Emergency Department promptly.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rectal bleeding, unintentional weight loss, or a persistent new change in bowel habit

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; anxious about the abdomen
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear obstruction red flags and a definite review plan since no one else can observe deterioration.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Start the macrogol powder with plenty of water, use the after-breakfast toilet routine and a footstool, and arrange a review in the next day or two. It should work over 24–48 hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Start laxative + GP review within 24–48h; ED for obstruction/peritonism features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED if flatus stops, you vomit, or the abdomen becomes rigid; otherwise manage overnight with the laxative.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Functional constipation with faecal loading; obstruction and alarm features screened negative remotely; macrogol started; confirm exam/DRE and arrange screening.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Laxatives with no obstruction screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A true obstruction is treated as constipation, stimulant laxatives are given, and the bowel perforates.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No alarm-feature screen / no prompt review after 6 days of severe symptoms.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for benign functional constipation with a reassuring obstruction screen — usually unnecessary.
